name: api-routes
context: Build API endpoints in Next.js â€” Route Handlers (App Router) and API Routes (Pages Router) for REST, webhooks, and backends
info: Next.js provides two ways to build API endpoints. In the App Router, Route Handlers are defined in `route.ts` files that export HTTP method functions (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS, HEAD). They receive a standard `Request` object (or `NextRequest` for additional helpers) and return a `Response` (or `NextResponse`). In the Pages Router, API routes live in `pages/api/` as default-exported handler functions receiving `(req, res)` objects. Both support dynamic routes, middleware, streaming responses, and edge runtime. Route Handlers are the recommended approach for new projects â€” they align with Web API standards and support streaming, but Pages Router API routes remain stable for existing codebases.
triggers: create an API endpoint in next.js
build a REST API with next

sections.title: Route Handlers (App Router)
sections.body: Route Handlers are defined in `app/**/route.ts` files. Export named functions matching HTTP methods: `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`, `OPTIONS`, `HEAD`. They receive a standard `Request` (or `NextRequest`) and return a `Response` (or `NextResponse`). `route.ts` cannot coexist with `page.tsx` in the same directory. Static Route Handlers (GET without reading request) are cached by default â€” opt out with `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'`.

sections.title: API Routes (Pages Router)
sections.body: API routes live in `pages/api/` and export a default handler function `(req: NextApiRequest, res: NextApiResponse) => void`. Route based on `req.method`. Access query params via `req.query`, body via `req.body` (auto-parsed JSON). Dynamic routes use `[param].ts` naming. Disable body parsing with `export const config = { api: { bodyParser: false } }`.

sections.title: Streaming responses
sections.body: Route Handlers support streaming via Web Streams API. Create a `ReadableStream`, enqueue chunks with `controller.enqueue()`, and return `new Response(stream)`. Useful for server-sent events, large file downloads, and AI response streaming. Set appropriate `Content-Type` headers (`text/event-stream` for SSE, `application/x-ndjson` for newline-delimited JSON).

sections.title: CORS and custom headers
sections.body: Handle CORS by exporting an `OPTIONS` handler that returns appropriate `Access-Control-Allow-*` headers. For Route Handlers, use `NextResponse` or configure CORS in [[nextjs/middleware]] for app-wide coverage. For Pages Router, set headers with `res.setHeader()`. Custom response headers can be set via `NextResponse.headers.set()` or in `next.config.js` headers configuration.
